message:
feat(wal,core,e2e): rename dist_* to hist_*, add WAL commit-path profile to report, fix hot-path profiling isolation and ORDER BY alias resolution

- wal/group_commit: rename snapshot fields from dist_* to hist_* for naming
  consistency; derive Serialize/Deserialize on PhasePercentiles,
  WakeReasonSnapshot, ConsolidationMetricsSnapshot; make PhaseHistogram::new
  pub const
- wal/commit_path_histograms: replace standalone unit tests with global-singleton
  tests that exercise all nine histogram fields and wake-reason counters via
  GLOBAL_CONSOLIDATION_METRICS
- core/connection: lower MAX_TRIGGER_DEPTH 100→32 (~20-40 KiB stack per level);
  add thread-local hot-path profile override for test isolation so concurrent
  test threads are not accidentally profiled; fix vtab registry cleanup and
  time-travel snapshot capture on commit_and_retain fast path; release cached
  write/read txns before integrity-check and checkpoint to avoid Busy lock
- core/connection: fix ORDER BY alias resolution to check alias before column
  name (SELECT cat AS c … ORDER BY c now works correctly)
- e2e/report: add WalCommitPathProfile with all nine phase histograms and wake
  reasons; embed it in WalHotPathProfile behind #[serde(default)]
- e2e/perf_runner: add HotPathCausalEvidence, HotPathCausalBucketEntry,
  HotPathCausalClassificationSummary structs; wire causal_classification and
  causal_buckets into HotPathActionableRanking; add WalHotPathProfile field to
  HotPathSubsystemProfilePack
- e2e/bd_3wop3_7: clarify that throughput gates in this file are historical
  scaffolding only; rename placeholder helper to make its non-truthful nature
  explicit

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","title":"A6.2: define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles","description":"## Goal
define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles

## Checklist
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The execution contract defines baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles with exact rules for CPU pinning, SMT packing or spreading, NUMA or memory policy, helper-lane placement, and any required environment disclosures.
2. Each profile includes a user-facing invocation contract showing how suites and focused reruns select it, which knobs are fixed versus optional, and when the profile is mandatory for a claim.
3. The contract is stable enough that G7 suites, Track H comparison work, and future operators can reproduce placement-sensitive runs without hidden tribal knowledge.
4. The result makes it clear when a run violated the placement contract and therefore cannot be compared cleanly.","notes":"Execution-contract obligations:
- define baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles concretely enough that humans and agents can invoke them without guesswork
- specify CPU pinning, SMT policy, memory placement, helper-lane placement, and required environment disclosures for each profile
- align profile naming and run identity with the stable artifact naming/provenance contract so operators can predict artifact locations and compare like with like
- include operator guidance on when each profile is required, optional, or intentionally avoided
- optimize for users by preventing false comparisons caused by silent drift in pinning or memory policy between runs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:44.131929556Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T07:47:40.237157184Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T07:26:25.061417210Z","close_reason":"Completed: exact baseline/recommended/adversarial placement execution contract added to the live campaign manifest/types/docs with targeted verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","parallelism","perf","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:22.293473399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2372,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed after closing bd-db300.1.4.2 via fresh bv robot triage. Starting the execution-contract slice by reading the existing topology discovery bundle, placement-profile taxonomy, fixture selection surfaces, and artifact naming contract so baseline/recommended/adversarial profile rules land in the canonical reusable surface instead of scattered notes.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:00:13Z"},{"id":2373,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Completed the placement execution contract in the live benchmark campaign surface. Added a typed execution_contract for baseline_unpinned, recommended_pinned, and adversarial_cross_node in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs; updated sample_sqlite_db_files/manifests/beads_benchmark_campaign.v1.json and .schema.json with exact CPU affinity / SMT / memory / helper-lane / claim / rerun-binding rules; and documented the contract in crates/fsqlite-e2e/RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md. Also fixed an unrelated clippy lock-scope issue in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs uncovered during verification. Verification: rustfmt on touched Rust files; python3 -m json.tool on both campaign JSON files; rch cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e test_beads_benchmark_campaign_placement_execution_contract_is_explicit -- --nocapture; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e test_beads_benchmark_campaign_manifest_matches_json_schema -- --nocapture; rch cargo fmt --check; and rch cargo clippy -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings. Full clippy without --no-deps still encounters a pre-existing unrelated ref_option lint in crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:26:46Z"},{"id":2376,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up: benchmark artifact manifests were only carrying a thin placement_policy, so evidence bundles were not self-describing for the new placement execution contract. Propagated execution_contract into fixture_select artifact provenance + shared JSON schema, and kept realdb_e2e schema tests remote-safe under rch by retaining embedded schema fallbacks while validating local tracked-schema parity when available. Verification: cargo fmt --check, rustfmt --check on touched Rust files, python3 -m json.tool on beads_benchmark_artifact_manifest.v1.schema.json, and rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_db300_fresh_eyes_a163 cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e artifact_manifest -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:47:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","title":"A6.3: capture IRQ helper-lane and memory-policy side conditions","description":"## Goal
capture IRQ helper-lane and memory-policy side conditions

## Checklist
1. Record IRQ placement, helper-lane placement, NUMA allocation policy, interleave or membind behavior, and automatic NUMA balancing state.
2. Distinguish intentional from accidental settings.
3. Make the data consumable by tuning and verification work.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Record IRQ placement, helper-lane placement, NUMA allocation policy, interleave or membind behavior, and automatic NUMA balancing state.
2. Distinguish intentional from accidental settings.
3. Make the data consumable by tuning and verification work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. IRQ placement, helper-lane placement, NUMA memory policy, interleave or membind behavior, and automatic NUMA balancing state are captured in a way that is comparable across runs and profiles.
2. The bead distinguishes intentional configuration from incidental environment drift and records enough context to explain when a side condition plausibly changed a result.
3. The resulting fields and artifact hooks are directly usable by topology-stress suites, failure triage, and operator reruns without additional ad hoc investigation.
4. The side-condition contract is explicit enough that future benchmark claims can say whether helper-lane or memory-policy effects were controlled, tolerated, or suspected.","notes":"Side-condition obligations:
- record IRQ placement, helper-lane placement, memory policy, interleave or membind state, and automatic NUMA balancing in a stable comparable form
- distinguish deliberate configuration from accidental drift so later triage can tell whether a regression was code-induced or environment-induced
- require enough run metadata that topology-stress and failure bundles can explain suspicious results without fresh archaeology
- optimize for users by making side-condition surprises visible early instead of after a misleading benchmark claim has already circulated","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:58.121683842Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:58:27.597395312Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:58:27.401483570Z","close_reason":"Completed side-condition capture and artifact-manifest persistence hook","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","mechanical-sympathy","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:47:41.754187094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2374,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed immediately after closing bd-db300.1.6.2. Starting the side-condition follow-on by reusing the new A6.2 placement execution contract as the canonical anchor for IRQ helper-lane and memory-policy evidence capture.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:27:38Z"},{"id":2378,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation at scripts/verify_bd_db300_1_6_1_topology_bundle.sh. Plan: add stable side-condition capture for IRQ placement, helper-lane intent vs observation, and NUMA memory-policy/interleave state, then evaluate whether artifact provenance/schema needs a follow-on extension.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:52:06Z"},{"id":2379,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented the first live A6.3 capture pass in scripts/verify_bd_db300_1_6_1_topology_bundle.sh. The bundle now emits a structured side_conditions section with placement-contract inference, cpu_affinity_mask/helper_lane_cpu_set/memory_policy/numa_balancing_state disclosures, IRQ affinity + irqbalance state, and an overall comparability_assessment (controlled/tolerated/suspected/insufficient_evidence). Added the BENCHMARK_PID safeguard so declared pinned worker placement is not falsely marked drift just because the capture script itself was launched unpinned.","created_at":"2026-03-15T08:02:00Z"},{"id":2385,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended the shared artifact contract so hardware discovery / side-condition filenames are now first-class manifest fields. Updated BenchmarkArtifactContract + BenchmarkArtifactNames, the checked-in campaign manifest/schema, the shared artifact-manifest schema, the embedded realdb_e2e schema fallback, and the targeted fixture_select assertions. The topology-bundle script now honors MANIFEST_JSON artifact_names.hardware_discovery_* when present, so the manifest hook is not just documentation.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:56:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.7","title":"A7: refresh authoritative c1 and persistent 16t evidence around the actual 2026-03-23 failures","description":"## Goal
Refresh the authoritative evidence surface around the two failures that matter most right now: the `c1` fixed-tax problem and the persistent 8t/16t commit-path collapse.

Why this bead exists now:
- The latest canonical matrix shows MVCC and forced single-writer are almost identical at `c1`, so the first-order problem there is core engine service time rather than MVCC conflict logic.
- The persistent writer benchmark still falls off a cliff by 16 threads, so the many-writer story needs its own evidence lane.
- The repo already has useful counters and older artifacts, but future work needs a current, authoritative pack focused on the exact failing cells rather than relying on older mixed hot-cell captures.

This bead is the measurement anchor for the current overlay. It should produce the evidence packs and classification needed by the low-tax, commit-collapse, advanced-decision, and final-scorecard lanes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:22:43.425791274Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:30:18.691080448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","overlay-2026-03-23","profiling","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:22:43.425791274Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2534,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7","author":"codex","text":"## Intent of A7

`A7` is the evidence-refresh lane. It exists because the 2026-03-23 results changed the story: the project is not looking at one generic “performance gap,” but at a split failure signature with one low-concurrency disease and one many-writer tail-collapse disease.

The deliverables in this lane should leave behind authoritative artifact packs for the exact red cells, not just rerun logs. At minimum the packs should carry:
- benchmark command lines, toolchain/build profile, machine context, and fixture identity,
- throughput plus p50/p95/p99/max latency where applicable,
- top CPU and allocation profiles for the worst `c1` cells,
- phase-attribution and wake-path evidence for persistent `8t` and `16t`,
- an explicit classification of each red cell into service, queueing, synchronization, allocation, or mixed causes.

## Why this track is first

The previous local matrix makes two facts too strong to ignore:
- `c1` MVCC and single-writer are nearly identical, so low-concurrency underperformance is not primarily a page-conflict or multi-writer story.
- Persistent `16t` collapses far harder than `8t`, which is typical of a convoy or publication pathology, not just “a bit more work.”

This lane therefore exists to freeze the real before-state and prevent the project from optimizing against folklore. It follows the extreme-software-optimization rule: baseline first, profile first, and re-profile after each shift because the dominant bottleneck will move.

## Reading the child beads

- `A7.1` owns authoritative `c1` hot-path packs.
- `A7.2` owns authoritative persistent `8t/16t` phase packs.
- `A7.3` converts raw evidence into causal classification.
- `A7.4` freezes provenance and scorecard conventions so later reruns remain comparable.

A future agent should treat this lane as the ground truth source for the rest of the overlay. If these packs are weak, every downstream conclusion is weak.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:30:18Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","title":"A7.1: capture authoritative c1 hot-path artifact packs for the worst low-concurrency cells","description":"Capture fresh `release-perf` artifact packs for the critical low-concurrency cells that currently prove FrankenSQLite has a large fixed service-time tax.

Minimum scope:
- the worst `c1` cells across the canonical workloads and fixtures;
- direct SQLite control runs;
- MVCC and forced single-writer runs;
- enough hot-path detail that later beads can attribute the fixed-tax problem to named subsystems rather than generic wall time.

This bead should foreground the cells where MVCC and single-writer are nearly identical, because those are the cells that prove the baseline engine tax is dominating before writer concurrency becomes relevant.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:23:16.150089835Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:27:37.944934587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","c1","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","overlay-2026-03-23","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:23:16.150089835Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2555,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

This bead should leave behind the canonical low-concurrency evidence pack, not just a benchmark rerun. At minimum it should capture stable command lines, workload identity, fixture identity, build profile, profiler artifacts, counter snapshots, and the exact c1 cells used as the reference red cases. The output should make it cheap for a future user to answer: "what is the low-tax problem made of right now?"
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:47:10Z"},{"id":2587,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: reproducibility pack for c1 evidence

This bead should leave behind a reproducibility pack that a future agent can run without re-reading session history. In addition to the hot artifacts themselves, the pack should include stable entrypoints, exact fixture identity, build profile, seeds if any, and machine-readable metadata such as `env.json`, `manifest.json`, or equivalent provenance files.

The pack should also preserve the baseline comparator explicitly: which SQLite run or artifact is being compared, which FrankenSQLite mode is being compared, and where the before-state checksums or profile references live.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:20Z"},{"id":2604,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claiming bd-db300.1.7.1. Building c1 evidence packs: worst low-concurrency cells, release-perf entrypoints, build profile + fixture identity + hot-path artifact locations.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:23:25Z"},{"id":2605,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Progress (2026-03-23)

### Entrypoint
`scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh` — captures c1 evidence for worst cells.

### Worst c1 cells (from performance program):
1. commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c1 = 0.205x (5x slower than SQLite)
2. hot_page_contention c1 = 0.633x (1.6x slower)
3. mixed_read_write c1 = 3.519x (already winning — included for comparison)

### Script captures:
- C SQLite control (c1)
- FrankenSQLite MVCC (c1)
- FrankenSQLite single-writer (c1)
- Hot-path profile for worst cell (commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys)
- Build metadata (profile, git sha, CPU, hostname)

### Build
release-perf build in progress: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/c1-evidence cargo build --profile release-perf -p fsqlite-e2e --bin realdb-e2e`
","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:27:37Z"}]}

","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:23:25Z"},{"id":2605,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Progress (2026-03-23)

### Entrypoint
`scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh` — captures c1 evidence for worst cells.

### Worst c1 cells (from performance program):
1. commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c1 = 0.205x (5x slower than SQLite)
2. hot_page_contention c1 = 0.633x (1.6x slower)
3. mixed_read_write c1 = 3.519x (already winning — included for comparison)

### Script captures:
- C SQLite control (c1)
- FrankenSQLite MVCC (c1)
- FrankenSQLite single-writer (c1)
- Hot-path profile for worst cell (commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys)
- Build metadata (profile, git sha, CPU, hostname)

### Build
release-perf build in progress: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/c1-evidence cargo build --profile release-perf -p fsqlite-e2e --bin realdb-e2e`
","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:27:37Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Closure-ready evidence (2026-03-23)

### Entrypoint
`scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh` — self-contained, records build metadata, runs all 3 engines × 3 workloads + hot-path profile.

### Run
```
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/c1-evidence ./scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh /tmp/c1_evidence_pack
```

### Authoritative c1 ratios (release-perf, fixture=frankensqlite, median ops/s)

| Workload | SQLite | MVCC | Single-writer | MVCC/SQLite | SW/SQLite | MVCC/SW |
|----------|--------|------|---------------|-------------|-----------|---------|
| commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys | 92.0 | 51.7 | 56.0 | **0.562x** | 0.609x | 0.924x |
| hot_page_contention | 2239.8 | 2512.5 | 130.7 | **1.122x** | 0.058x | 19.2x |
| mixed_read_write | 1252.3 | 8378.8 | 8670.5 | **6.691x** | 6.923x | 0.966x |

### Key findings
1. **Worst c1 cell**: commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys at 0.562x (improved from 0.205x reported 2 days ago — prior optimizations working).
2. **MVCC overhead is small**: MVCC/SW ratios are 0.92-0.97 for insert/mixed — the engine tax dominates, not MVCC.
3. **hot_page_contention anomaly**: Single-writer is catastrophically slow (0.058x) while MVCC wins (1.12x). The SW path has a pathological contention mode.
4. **mixed_read_write dominance**: 6.7x — the read-heavy workload is already a clear win.

### Artifact pack
`/tmp/c1_evidence_pack/` contains:
- `build_metadata.json` — profile, git sha, CPU, hostname
- `c1_sqlite3.jsonl` — C SQLite control
- `c1_fsqlite_mvcc.jsonl` — FrankenSQLite MVCC
- `c1_fsqlite_single.jsonl` — FrankenSQLite single-writer
- `c1_hotprofile_commutative.log` — hot-path profile for worst cell

### Hot-path attribution (commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c1)
From hot-profile: parse=3759 SQL bytes, row_materialization=2363ns, mvcc_page_lock_wait=0ns. The fixed cost is in the parse→compile→execute ceremony, not MVCC.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T22:01:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","title":"A7.2: capture authoritative persistent 8t and 16t phase-attribution packs for the commit path","description":"Capture fresh persistent-writer artifact packs for 8-thread and 16-thread runs using the current commit-path counters and any improved distributions produced by the commit-path lane.

Required outputs:
- phase-attribution evidence for both 8t and 16t;
- tail statistics rather than only medians or means;
- throughput, retry or conflict behavior, and wake/publication evidence;
- exact build and environment provenance.

This is the authoritative evidence bead for the persistent thesis benchmark and should become the default reference for all later commit-path claims.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:23:16.722721458Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:20.909077175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["16t","benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","db300-track-c","evidence","overlay-2026-03-23","persistent-bench","track-c","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:23:16.722721458Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:02.109105364Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:02.600363530Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2556,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

This bead should act like the canonical persistent-benchmark evidence bundle, not just a benchmark rerun. It should preserve one-command replay entrypoints, the exact thread counts and workload shape, structured phase summaries, and machine-readable provenance artifacts so later causal packs and gates can consume the results without bespoke parsing.

Where feasible, the pack should also make the reference comparator explicit: which prior persistent pack or SQLite baseline is being compared and what verdict each critical thread regime produced.

 {"id":"bd-db300.3.6","title":"C6: remove page-1 allocator and freelist conflict surface from logically disjoint writers","description":"## Goal
Eliminate the remaining page-1/freelist conflict surface that still makes logically disjoint writers collide in the canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` benchmark. The current profiler evidence shows `allocate_page` and `free_page` still track `PageNumber::ONE` as a conflict-only page, so writers that should be independent converge on a shared hotspot before commit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The conditions that truly require page-1/freelist conflict tracking are explicit and tested.
- Logically disjoint insert workloads no longer collide on `PageNumber::ONE` unless correctness requires it.
- Canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` reruns show materially lower retry/abort pressure and better throughput.
- Targeted unit tests and canonical-matrix e2e coverage prove correctness and measure the improvement.
- Structured profiling artifacts tie before/after results to the exact workload, fixture, and concurrency cells.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-c","hot-path","pager","perf","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3.7","title":"Eliminate residual page-1 conflict-only tracking from disjoint writer path","description":"## Goal
Stop forcing `PageNumber::ONE` into the MVCC write-conflict surface for logically disjoint insert/free workloads unless page-1 metadata is semantically changed. The current evidence pack for `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c8` shows catastrophic slowdown while the hottest path is `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page`, and the allocate/free path still calls `track_concurrent_conflict_only_page(..., PageNumber::ONE, ...)` in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` before pager allocation/free completes.

## Evidence
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:1308` tracks `PageNumber::ONE` on `allocate_page`.
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:1374` tracks `PageNumber::ONE` on `free_page`.
- `artifacts/perf/20260313_deep_profile/disjoint_c8_fsqlite_mvcc.perfreport.txt` shows ~95% of sampled cycles in `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page` with retry/conflict machinery visible.
- `artifacts/perf/20260313_deep_profile/disjoint_c8_fsqlite_mvcc.stdout.json` shows ~22.3 ops/s, 144 retries, and 35.9s wall time on the disjoint c8 cell.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The exact semantic conditions under which page-1 must enter the conflict surface are explicit and tested.
2. Disjoint insert/free paths no longer create false page-1 conflicts when page-1 metadata is not actually mutated.
3. Targeted regression tests cover allocate/free success, rollback, and crash-safe restoration semantics.
4. The canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` hot cell is re-profiled with before/after artifacts showing whether retries, instructions/op, and throughput improve.
5. Any remaining page-1 conflict cases are documented with rationale rather than hidden inside generic helper calls.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build the authoritative perf binary used for focused MVCC comparison runs.
2. Run the canonical commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c8 slice on frankensqlite with sqlite3 and fsqlite_mvcc controls.
3. Capture perf report/stat output plus benchmark stdout artifacts before and after the change.
4. Compare page-1 touch count, retry causes, instructions per op, and throughput deltas to confirm whether false page-1 conflicts actually fell.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The exact semantic conditions under which page-1 must enter the conflict surface are explicit and tested.
2. Disjoint insert/free paths no longer create false page-1 conflicts when page-1 metadata is not actually mutated.
3. Targeted regression tests cover allocate/free success, rollback, and crash-safe restoration semantics.
4. The canonical commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys hot cell is re-profiled with before/after artifacts showing whether retries, instructions/op, and throughput improve.
5. Any remaining page-1 conflict cases are documented with rationale rather than hidden inside generic helper calls.","notes":"## Steps to Reproduce
1. Build the authoritative perf binary used for focused MVCC comparison runs.
2. Run the canonical commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c8 slice on frankensqlite with sqlite3 and fsqlite_mvcc controls.
3. Capture perf report/stat output plus benchmark stdout artifacts before and after the change.
4. Compare page-1 touch count, retry causes, instructions per op, and throughput deltas to confirm whether false page-1 conflicts actually fell.

## Verification Notes
- preserve before/after artifacts under the canonical db300 evidence layout
- include allocate/free rollback and crash-safe restoration regressions in the proof pack","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:24.407945139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:06.329187097Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-c","hot-path","mvcc","pager","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:24.407945139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3.8","title":"C8: eliminate the 16-thread persistent commit-path tail collapse before adding more writers","description":"## Goal
Eliminate the persistent 16-thread commit-path tail collapse that is erasing FrankenSQLite's many-writer advantage exactly where the project is supposed to win hardest.

Why this bead exists now:
- The persistent benchmark is strong at 2t and 4t, near parity at 8t, and catastrophic at 16t. That pattern points to a queueing, synchronization, or publication pathology, not a uniform engine slowdown.
- Current code surfaces worth suspecting include the fixed arrival-wait loop, the condvar plus timeout waiter publication path, flusher-side frame assembly work, and the shared WAL backend write-lock scope.
- Existing phase timing only reports averages, which is not enough for a tail problem.

This bead is the commit-collapse recovery lane. Its job is to make the tail visible, remove convoy sources, shrink or clarify serialized scopes, and only then decide whether more advanced durability-lane machinery is justified.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:22:44.352280486Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:14.041461257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["16t","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-c","group-commit","overlay-2026-03-23","persistent-bench","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:22:44.352280486Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2536,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8","author":"codex","text":"## Intent of C8

`C8` is the many-writer commit-path collapse lane. It exists because the persistent synthetic benchmark is not merely “behind” at high threads; it falls off a cliff.

The baseline this lane is anchored to is:
- `2t` about `5.51x` SQLite,
- `4t` about `3.68x`,
- `8t` about `0.98x`,
- `16t` about `0.18x`.

That shape strongly suggests queueing amplification, wakeup pathology, or lock-topology collapse. It does not look like a simple linear increase in useful work.

## Suspected first-order subsystems

Current code inspection and phase logs make the following surfaces suspicious:
- the waiter publication path around the group-commit epoch and condvar discipline,
- the fixed `20us` arrival-wait heuristic in the flusher path,
- the broad shared WAL backend write-lock scope,
- flusher-side batch-assembly work before append,
- anything that turns rare slow wakeups into convoy behavior across many waiting writers.

Relevant implementation surfaces are primarily `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs` and `crates/fsqlite-wal/src/group_commit.rs`.

## How this lane uses advanced ideas without turning reckless

This track is allowed to use strong ideas from the alien-graveyard and alien-artifact toolkits, but only behind evidence and fallbacks.

The motivating graveyard concepts here are the ones that actually match the symptom profile:
- tail-latency decomposition rather than average-only reasoning,
- publication primitives such as eventcount, RCU/QSBR, and seqlock-style read-mostly metadata techniques where correctness permits,
- queueing- and control-theoretic treatment of batching policies,
- parallel WAL or segmented durability lanes only if simpler convoy fixes still leave the regime red.

The graph deliberately keeps the advanced durability-lane card late. The first responsibility of this lane is to prove or falsify the simpler convoy hypotheses.

## Reading the child beads

- `C8.1` and `C8.2` improve observability so the tails are visible.
- `C8.3` audits the current publication path for convoy behavior.
- `C8.4` is the likely first architectural intervention if the audit confirms the wake path is sick.
- `C8.5`, `C8.6`, and `C8.7` target the arrival heuristic, pre-append service work, and lock scope.
- `C8.8` is the causal rerun ledger.
- `C8.9` is the advanced segmented durability-lane card and should only activate if the simpler path fails.
- `C8.10` makes crash/corruption/fairness proof work explicit for any new primitive.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:30:19Z"},{"id":2545,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: explicit commit-path test and logging lane

Track C8 now carries explicit validation beads `C8.11` and `C8.12`, and `C8.10` is intentionally downstream of those plus the causal rerun ledger. This makes the commit-path lane safer to operate in plan space: synchronization and durability-adjacent changes are not considered complete until they have direct unit or concurrency coverage, persistent e2e stress scripts, and artifact-rich logging.

Future agents should read `C8.10` as the point where the lane proves not just that throughput changed, but that crash, corruption, fairness, and wake-path behavior stayed acceptable under the rewritten commit path.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:14Z"}]}

- `wal_backend_lock_wait`,

- wakeup-to-run latency,
- wake reasons such as notify, timeout, flusher takeover, failed epoch, and busy-retry.

This is the bead that makes the likely culprit subsystems mechanically visible enough to rank, not merely discuss.

","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:53:34Z"},{"id":2606,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## C8.1 Implementation — Per-Phase Distributions + Wake-Reason Accounting

### What was implemented (in `crates/fsqlite-wal/src/group_commit.rs`)

**1. `PhaseHistogram` struct** — lock-free ring buffer (4096 samples) with:
- `record(value_us)`: atomic write_idx advance + sample store + running max CAS + sum accumulation
- `percentiles()` → `PhasePercentiles { p50, p95, p99, max, count, mean_us }`
- Concurrent-writer safe (no locks, only atomic ops on hot path)
- `reset()` for test isolation

**2. `WakeReasonCounters` struct** — 5 atomic counters:
- `notify`, `timeout`, `flusher_takeover`, `failed_epoch`, `busy_retry`
- `snapshot()` → `WakeReasonSnapshot` with `.total()`
- `reset()` for test isolation

**3. ConsolidationMetrics gains 9 histograms + 1 wake-reason tracker:**
- `hist_consolidator_lock_wait` — every commit
- `hist_arrival_wait` — flusher only
- `hist_wal_backend_lock_wait` — flusher only (inner lock)
- `hist_wal_append` — flusher only
- `hist_exclusive_lock` — flusher only
- `hist_wal_sync` — flusher only
- `hist_waiter_epoch_wait` — waiter only
- `hist_phase_b` — every commit (full group-commit phase)
- `hist_full_commit` — every commit (phase A+B+C)
- `wake_reasons` — waiter wake accounting

**4. Wired into `record_phase_timing()`** — each call records to the corresponding histogram alongside the existing total accumulators. No existing behavior changed.

**5. Wired into `record_commit_phases()`** — records to `hist_full_commit`.

**6. `ConsolidationMetricsSnapshot` extended** with 9 `PhasePercentiles` fields + `WakeReasonSnapshot`. `snapshot()` and `reset()` updated.

**7. Exported from `fsqlite_wal::lib.rs`**: `PhaseHistogram`, `PhasePercentiles`, `WakeReasonCounters`, `WakeReasonSnapshot`.

**8. Tests added** (10 new tests):
- `phase_histogram_empty_returns_zeros`
- `phase_histogram_single_sample`
- `phase_histogram_percentiles_ordered`
- `phase_histogram_max_tracks_outlier`
- `phase_histogram_reset_clears_state`
- `phase_histogram_concurrent_writers_no_panic` (4 threads × 500 samples)
- `wake_reason_counters_track_all_reasons`
- `wake_reason_reset_clears`
- `consolidation_metrics_snapshot_includes_distributions`

### What's NOT done yet
- Wire `wake_reasons` counters into the actual waiter wakeup sites in pager.rs (the counters exist but the pager commit path doesn't increment them yet — requires editing pager.rs which other agents may be touching)
- Exposing distributions via PRAGMA or virtual table

### Compilation
- `cargo check -p fsqlite-wal` passes cleanly
- Tests compiling (cold cache, link in progress)
","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:59:14Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","title":"C8.11: add comprehensive unit and concurrency tests with structured wake and lock diagnostics for commit-path rewrites","description":"Build the commit-path unit and concurrency verification layer for epoch publication, wake behavior, arrival policy changes, lock-scope narrowing, and flusher batch-assembly rewrites.

This bead exists because the C8 lane is altering synchronization and durability-adjacent behavior. Every such change needs direct tests that can fail loudly with useful diagnostics instead of relying on a later benchmark anomaly to hint that something broke.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:39:53.958690129Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:21.543167191Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["16t","db300-program","db300-track-c","logging","overlay-2026-03-23","testing","track-c","unit-tests","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:39:53.958690129Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:47:08.762983552Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:02.579216122Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:03.221722864Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:03.838715815Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:04.478125148Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2550,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

The local verification surface here should cover notifier vs timeout wake paths, epoch advancement, lock-wait accounting, arrival-policy decisions, and batch-assembly invariants for the commit path. Failure logs should preserve enough epoch, wake, and lock context that convoy or publication bugs can be distinguished from plain service-time regressions.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:15Z"},{"id":2591,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: proof and interference discipline

This bead should not stop at unit and concurrency tests. For any composed commit-path change, especially publication rewrites plus adaptive arrival logic, it should require an explicit interference check and a short behavior-preservation or safety note explaining why the combination is expected to remain sane.

That note should identify the conservative fallback path, the disable trigger, and any timescale-separation or controller-interaction assumptions that later operators must preserve.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","title":"C8.12: add persistent 2t/4t/8t/16t e2e stress scripts with crash, fairness, corruption, and detailed phase logging","description":"Add one-command persistent end-to-end stress and crash or fairness scripts for the commit-path lane, with detailed structured logs and reproducible artifact capture across 2t, 4t, 8t, and 16t.

This bead turns the persistent writer thesis benchmark into a reusable operator-quality verification surface rather than a single ad hoc rerun. It should capture enough detail that a future failure can be diagnosed from artifacts instead of memory.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:39:54.656614254Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:21.754429134Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["16t","benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-c","e2e","fairness","logging","overlay-2026-03-23","persistent-bench","testing","track-c","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:05.785639021Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:19.592523326Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:39:54.656614254Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:05.129810646Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2551,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

The scripts here should act like a reusable persistent-writer proving ground: 2t/4t anchors, 8t and 16t focus, crash and recovery verdicts, corruption verdicts, fairness summaries, wake or phase histograms, reproducibility metadata, and stable artifact locations. The point is to make commit-path regressions mechanically diagnosable.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:15Z"},{"id":2592,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.8.12","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: persistent script artifact contract

These scripts should emit an operator-quality artifact bundle, not just terminal output. The bundle should include stable file locations, machine-readable summaries, structured logs, thread-count-specific verdicts, and enough provenance to replay the exact run later.

Because this bead now depends on `A7.4`, the script outputs should align with the frozen provenance and scorecard contract rather than inventing a private reporting format.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:21Z"}]}
